steps.title: Fit the labor-only cost
steps.content: Ignore parts for one moment and find just the labor charge. One hour costs 112 dollars, so use $(1,112)$. Two hours costs $112+40=152$ dollars, so use $(2,152)$. The hourly labor rate is $$\frac{152-112}{2-1}=40.$$

steps.title: Add the parts cost
steps.content: The fitted labor line is $y=40x+72$. Parts add an independent cost of $z$, so the total is $$y=40x+72+z,$$ or $$y=40x+z+72.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice B is $y=40x+z+72$. It charges 112 dollars for the first hour because $40(1)+72=112$, adds 40 dollars for each additional hour, and then adds the parts cost $z$. Choice B is correct.
